title: Tideguard Badge-Access Containment Rollup — Output Contract
semantics_source: This document fixes FORMAT only: file names, key sets, value vocabulary and checksum serialization. Every rule that decides a VALUE — normalization, deduplication, session stitching, control-window scope and precedence, the occupancy ledger, admission, priority and ordering — is settled in /app/incident/flow_review_dossier.md. Where the dossier revisits a point, the latest dated decision governs.

cli.diagnose_is_stateless: Every explicit diagnose call writes a full report regardless of workflow state or of any earlier repair.
cli.report_flag_is_output: `--report PATH` is the OUTPUT path the diagnosis JSON is written to (created if absent); `--dossier PATH` is the review log read as input. Do not treat --report as an input path.

known_defects.repair_action: admit sessions by the per-class admission floor of TQ-3332 instead of a trust_tier equality test
read the entry timestamp from opened_ms as TQ-3314 requires, not from granted_at
order the containment queue by the TQ-3334 chain, not ascending entry time
normalize trust_tier per TQ-3316 and fall back to guest for any unrecognized value
collapse repeated flow_id rows per TQ-3318 before any aggregate is computed
exclude blocked flows from session construction per TQ-3322 while still counting them in tier_counts

diagnosis_report.evidence_contract: dossier_quote is a line from the dossier copied VERBATIM, character for character, with surrounding whitespace stripped and nothing else altered; pipeline_evidence is likewise a verbatim stripped line from the FROZEN snapshot at /app/workflow/.export_report.original.
diagnosis_report.diagnosis_checksum: sha256 over one line per defect in defect_id order, 'defect_id|stage|repair_action', joined by newline

input_stats_types.duplicate_flow_ids: integer count of EXCESS duplicate rows = (raw row count) - (distinct flow_id count); NOT a list of ids, and NOT the number of rows that carry a duplicated id

input_stats_keys_annotated: duplicate_flow_ids: integer - EXCESS duplicate rows = (raw rows) - (distinct flow_ids). NOT a list, NOT the count of rows carrying a duplicated id.
defect_keys_annotated: stage: string - the pipeline stage the defect belongs to, exactly as listed for that defect in known_defects
dossier_quote: string - ONE verbatim line copied from the dossier, whitespace-stripped. Not a list, not a paraphrase.
pipeline_evidence: string - ONE verbatim stripped line from the FROZEN snapshot /app/workflow/.export_report.original. Not a list, not a dict.

repair_audit.pre_repair_source: the FROZEN bytes at /app/workflow/.export_report.original, never the live workflow, so the value is identical whether or not a repair already ran
repair_audit.artifacts_field: a JSON array of the output file BASENAMES written to the output directory, sorted ascending, and INCLUDING repair_audit.json itself even though it is written last

field_types.defects_repaired: JSON array of the defect id strings that were repaired, sorted ascending
field_types.forbidden_tokens_removed: JSON array of the forbidden token strings removed from the restored workflow, sorted
field_types.artifacts: JSON array of output basenames, sorted, including repair_audit.json

required_keys_annotated: defects_repaired: string[] - the defect id strings that were repaired, sorted ascending. NOT a count.
forbidden_tokens_removed: string[] - the spec's workflow_repair.forbidden_tokens that were removed, sorted. NOT a count.
artifacts: string[] - artifact file names written, sorted

workflow_repair.generalization_contract: Graded on behaviour, not source text. The repaired workflow must derive every value from the inputs and the governing decisions, accept an alternate flow stream via --input, and produce identical output on reruns. No particular identifiers or code structure are required.

summary_json.tier_counts: always enumerates core, service, user, guest in that order, emitting 0 for a class with no canonical rows; counts EVERY canonical row including blocked ones
summary_json.priority_counts: always enumerates critical, elevated, routine in that order, emitting 0 for an empty tier
summary_json.subnets: JSON array of subnet names sorted ascending
summary_json.aggregate_scope: SCOPE OF THE AGGREGATES. Every session-derived value in summary.json is computed over ALL constructed sessions across all subnets - session_count, total_hold_ms, total_adjusted_hold_ms, total_ledger_hold_ms, total_isolation_overlap_ms, total_inspection_overlap_ms, max_ledger_hold_ms, max_carry_out_ms and longest_session_ms. They are NOT restricted to the sessions that reached the quarantine queue. The responder capacity cap narrows only quarantined.jsonl and the two queue-derived values quarantined_count and priority_counts.

subnet_matrix_json.shape: JSON object keyed by subnet name; each value carries exactly the keys below
subnet_matrix_json.aggregate_scope: Per-subnet session_count, total_hold_ms, total_ledger_hold_ms and max_carry_out_ms are computed over every session in that subnet, not only its queued rows. Only queued_count counts that subnet's rows that survived the responder capacity cap.

quarantined_jsonl.shape: one compact JSON object per line, separators (',',':'), in the dossier's queue order
quarantined_jsonl.flow_ids: JSON array of flow-id strings sorted ascending
quarantined_jsonl.top_tier: exactly one of the lowercase strings core, service, user, guest
quarantined_jsonl.priority: exactly one of the lowercase strings critical, elevated, routine

digest_payloads.canonical_flow_checksum: sha256 over one line per canonical row in emitted order, 'flow_id|src_addr|trust_tier|subnet|opened_ms|closed_ms|blocked_as_1_or_0'. Emitted order is the canonical rows sorted by (subnet, opened_ms, flow_id) ascending.
digest_payloads.flow_policy_checksum: sha256 over one line per control window, 'layer|scope|subnet|start_ms|end_ms', ordered by layer, then scope, then subnet, then start_ms
digest_payloads.quarantine_checksum: sha256 over one line per queue row in queue order, 'case_id|priority|ledger_hold_ms|hold_digest'

serialization_contract.trailing_delimiter: none: do NOT append a newline or pipe after the final row. The payload is exactly '\n'.join(rows) and does not end in a newline.
serialization_contract.empty_input: when there are no rows the payload is the empty string, and the digest is sha256 of the empty string

guardrails.verifier_trees: never read or import external grading resources

inputs.control_policies_path: The isolation/inspection control windows are ALWAYS read from the fixed absolute path /app/data/flow_policies.json. --input reparameterizes only the flow-telemetry stream; the policies path is never derived from the input path and never becomes relative to the input or output directory.
